meta.title: Financial domain taxonomy (Level-1 knowledge anchors)
meta.scope: Universal banking: retail, corporate, transaction banking, global markets, investment banking, treasury, risk, and compliance as practiced at institutions such as international commercial/investment banks (e.g. Standard Chartered–style universal bank). Not limited to a single IT system or geography.
meta.design_principle: Level-1 domains describe **what exists in the financial world** (objects, events, measures). Legacy `system_profiles` entries (crm / trading / credit) are **deployment bundles** that cherry-pick concepts from multiple domains; they are not substitutes for this taxonomy.
meta.level2_policy: Level-2 themes are **illustrative** and intentionally incomplete. Extend themes as you add concepts; do not require full coverage before shipping.
meta.future_binding: Planned: tag each `concepts.json` entry with `primary_domain_id` (optional) and optional `theme_ids`; validate references against this file.

level1_domains.title_zh: 参与方、法律主体与关系网络
level1_domains.summary: Who transacts: natural/legal persons, roles (borrower, issuer, clearing member), group structures, beneficial ownership, LEI-style identity, distribution and RM coverage.

level2_themes.notes: Individual/HNW; RM books; optional private banking overlays.
Corporate, FI, sovereign, NBFI; often multi-entity and cross-border.
Lead capture, scoring, nurture, campaign attribution; universal retail/corporate acquisition.
Relationship manager coverage, portfolio assignment; wealth, corporate, and branch distribution.

level1_domains.title_zh: 协议、产品与现金流条款
level1_domains.summary: What is contracted: products, legal agreements, facilities, limits-as-contracts, amortisation and coupon schedules, covenants, product features (not the market quote itself).

level2_themes.notes: Term loan, revolver, trade finance, project finance skeletons.
Wrappers linking client-facing product to internal books.

level1_domains.title_zh: 金融工具、标识与参考/市场数据
level1_domains.summary: What is traded or referenced: securities, derivatives, FX pairs, rates, indices, listings, identifiers (ISIN etc.), calendars and static reference. Distinct from **positions** (state) and **events** (flows).

level2_themes.notes: Cash bonds, equities, listed instruments.

level2_themes.title_zh: 衍生品与资金市场工具
level2_themes.notes: IRS/CCS, options, structured notes; MM instruments.

level1_domains.title_zh: 交易事件、市场事件与结算链路
level1_domains.summary: What happened: orders and instructions, market executions, payments, settlement legs, nostro/vostro activity, corporate actions processing, lifecycle events. Immutable or append-only **facts** (contrast ledger state).

level2_themes.notes: RTGS, ACH-style flows; cross-border payment chains.
Brokerage, DMA, algo order lifecycle; trade booking handoff to positions.
Appointments, entitlements, reminders, churn-style servicing; not tied to a single system.

level1_domains.summary: State of books: chart of accounts, balances, sub-ledger, trading/banking book positions, accruals, marks, PV/FV, performance and P&L attribution. Bridges **events** to **reporting**.

level2_themes.notes: Accrual accounting, NII-related balances.
Mark-to-market, desk positions, day P&L.

level1_domains.summary: How much risk and against what: credit/market/operational measures, limits and utilisation, internal/external ratings, collateral and haircut, EAD/EAD-style exposure, regulatory capital overlays (as **measures**, not raw contracts).

level2_themes.notes: PD/LGD-style stacks; wholesale & retail credit.
Sensitivities, VaR-like summaries at desk level.

level1_domains.summary: How the firm funds itself and manages liquidity: FTP, liquidity buffers, interbank and own issuance, ALM mismatch, LCR/NSFR-style **management** views (distinct from every operational payment in transaction domain).
typical_artifacts: internal funds transfer price curve usage

level2_themes.notes: Placements, repos at treasury level.
Mismatch buckets; FTP allocation concepts.

level1_domains.summary: How the firm stays within law and policy: KYC/AML, sanctions screening results, market conduct surveillance, regulatory returns and reconciliation to internal ledgers. Often **cross-cuts** party and transaction domains with dedicated **reporting** artefacts.

level2_themes.notes: Transaction monitoring, STR/SAR-style case concepts (jurisdiction-specific naming avoided).
Capital, liquidity, large exposures — as reporting facts.
CDD/EDD case workflow, sanctions runs; applies across onboarding and ongoing monitoring.

legacy_bundle_to_domains.description: Non-exclusive mapping from current `system_profiles` bundle ids to primary Level-1 domains they draw from. Used for documentation and future concept tagging; not a runtime dependency for schema generation today.
